description: The Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government is central to the mission-driven government, from fixing the foundations of an affordable home to handing power back to communities and rebuilding local governments. MHCLG is a ministerial department, supported by 19 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
The Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government is central to the mission-driven government, from fixing the foundations of an affordable home to handing power back to communities and rebuilding local governments.

MHCLG is a ministerial department, supported by 19 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Government funding to tackle antisemitism and support Jewish communities
ordered_featured_documents.title: Funding boost to help councils tackle antisemitism

ordered_featured_documents.summary: What the changes coming in from Friday 1st May mean for you - including the ban on Section 21 no fault evictions, and new rules on pets and rent increases
ordered_featured_documents.title: When will the Renters' Right Act come into force?

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The most historic changes to private renting in England now give 11 million tenants landmark new rights and protections that will transform their experiences.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Historic protections for renters in action across England

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The government has confirmed it will bring forward further reforms to Right to Buy.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Right to Buy overhaul to safeguard social housing

ordered_featured_documents.summary: More time for vulnerable people to pay their bill after missing one month’s payment
ordered_featured_documents.title: Council tax shake-up to protect the most vulnerable households

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Landmark changes to protect UK democracy from scourge of foreign actors and financial influence are set to come into force.

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary is:

  • Head of the Department, responsible for its overall leadership and effective day-to-day running
  • Chief Policy Adviser to the Secretary of State and the ministerial team, supporting them to develop and achieve their priorities
  • Principal Accounting Officer, responsible and accountable to Parliament for the stewardship of the department’s budget and for ensuring value for money

The Permanent Secretary is a member of the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities’ Board, which advises and supervises on strategic clarity, commercial sense, talented people, management information and delivering results.

She also attends the Audit and Risk Assurance Committee.

role_appointments.title: Sarah Healey CB CVO - Permanent Secretary

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary is responsible for the overall management and leadership of the department.

The Permanent Secretary leads DCMS civil servants that are responsible for the contribution of the arts, sport, tourism, digital, media and creative industries to economic growth. This includes enriching lives, promoting Britain to the world, delivery of major projects such as broadband rollout.

Department for Digital, Culture, Media & Sport and Civil Service Board

details.body:
The Director General for Social Security, Disability and Pensions is responsible for: 

  • the development and implementation of strategy and policy to improve outcomes for people receiving support from the social security system; and in particular for disabled people, people with health conditions, and pensioners and savers 

  • the Government’s overall social security system and welfare strategy  

  • Universal Credit policy and analysis, Housing policy and analysis, and Carers policy and analysis  

  • other policy areas including devolution  

  • DWP’s partnerships with arm’s length bodies.

role_appointments.title: Mo Baines - Lead Non-Executive Director

details.body: Non-Executive Directors (NEDs) are senior leaders from outside government who sit on the Departmental Board. They bring independent expertise, insight and challenge to help shape strategy, monitor performance, and ensure strong governance. While they do not take part in daily operations, their advice and oversight support ministers and officials in delivering the Department’s priorities effectively for the public.

details.body: The Chief Planner and Head of the Planning Profession advises ministers on policy and practice relating to town and country planning and high profile and complex planning cases. They are also responsible for urban design and placemaking policy, and for the government’s Planning Capacity and Capability Programme, which aims to equip local planning authorities to deliver planning reform and unlock housing delivery.

details.body:
The Secretary of State has overall responsibility for the Department for the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs. She has specific responsibilities for:

  • budget, including Official Development Assistance (ODA)
  • international relations
  • senior appointments
  • economic growth

ordered_roles.title: Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government

details.body:
Deputises for the Prime Minister, including:

  • On House of Commons business and at Prime Minister’s Questions
  • Domestically, including official events at which the PM is unable to attend, and chairing meetings or committees in his absence
  • Internationally, at diplomatic events where the PM is unable to attend, and generally acting in support of the Government’s international agenda.

Supports the Prime Minister on government policy and delivery, and in any other responsibility agreed.

Special responsibility for employment rights and Making Work Pay, including:

  • Chair of the Cabinet Committee on the Future of Work, ensuring cross-government delivery of the ‘New Deal for Working People’

Cross-governmental coordination of communities policy, including:

  • Taskforce on community cohesion

ordered_roles.title: Deputy Prime Minister

details.body:
The MHCLG Board’s role is to advise and support ministers on the operational implications and effectiveness of policy proposals and help turn policy from ideas into actions and results.

Part of MHCLG’s leadership team, non-executive board members help with that task.

The role of Lead Non-Executive Director involves:

  • meeting regularly with other non-execs
  • ensuring the Secretary of State is aware of any concerns
  • supporting the Secretary of State in his or her role as chair of the board
  • ensuring that the non-execs meet alone with the Secretary of State from time to time
  • liaising with the government-wide Lead Non-Executive Director

ordered_roles.title: Lead Non-Executive Director

details.body: Non-Executive Directors (NEDs) are senior leaders from outside government who sit on the Departmental Board. They bring independent expertise, insight and challenge to help shape strategy, monitor performance, and ensure strong governance. While they do not take part in daily operations, their advice and oversight support ministers and officials in delivering the Department’s priorities effectively for the public.

details.body:
The special envoy will:  

  • promote His Majesty’s Government’s (HMG) policy on post-Holocaust issues internationally, including raising awareness of the importance of Holocaust remembrance, education and research, working with ministers and officials to deliver HMG’s international post-Holocaust issues policy objectives 
  • work with international partners, other special envoys and experts on post-Holocaust issues including representing the UK in appropriate international fora including as Head of the UK Delegation to the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance and at the Special Envoy’s meeting on Antisemitism
  • press for progress internationally on Holocaust-era property and art restitution issues. Drive forward implementation of the Terezin Declaration, including through the implementation of the Washington Principles on Nazi-Confiscated Art
  • raise awareness of HMG’s international action on post-Holocaust issues with domestic stakeholders, including the Jewish community
  • represent the UK at Holocaust commemoration events both in the UK and internationally as necessary, including Holocaust Memorial Day, observed annually on 27 January

View the full terms of reference for the special envoy role.

ordered_roles.title: UK Special Envoy for Post-Holocaust Issues
Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State

details.body:
The special envoy will:  

  • promote His Majesty’s Government’s (HMG) policy on post-Holocaust issues internationally, including raising awareness of the importance of Holocaust remembrance, education and research, working with ministers and officials to deliver HMG’s international post-Holocaust issues policy objectives 
  • work with international partners, other special envoys and experts on post-Holocaust issues including representing the UK in appropriate international fora including as Head of the UK Delegation to the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance and at the Special Envoy’s meeting on Antisemitism
  • press for progress internationally on Holocaust-era property and art restitution issues. Drive forward implementation of the Terezin Declaration, including through the implementation of the Washington Principles on Nazi-Confiscated Art
  • raise awareness of HMG’s international action on post-Holocaust issues with domestic stakeholders, including the Jewish community
  • represent the UK at Holocaust commemoration events both in the UK and internationally as necessary, including Holocaust Memorial Day, observed annually on 27 January

View the full terms of reference for the special envoy role.

role.title: UK Special Envoy for Post-Holocaust Issues

role_appointments.title: Jon Pearce MP - United Kingdom Special Envoy for Post-Holocaust Issues
